activities_projects.title: Analyzing Amanda’s Emotions
activities_projects.objective: Explore Amanda’s feelings and reactions in depth.
steps: Identify lines where Amanda expresses frustration, longing, or daydreaming
Write 3–4 sentences explaining why she feels that way
Discuss how imagination helps her cope with restrictions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze character emotions and develop interpretive skills.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis, textual support, and clarity

activities_projects.title: Parent-Child Interaction Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand dynamics between Amanda and the parent.
steps: List all parental instructions and Amanda’s imagined responses
Compare and contrast the parent’s expectations with Amanda’s desires
Write a short paragraph analyzing the relationship
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recognize conflicts and expectations in parent-child relationships.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and depth of analysis

activities_projects.title: Imaginary Roles Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Examine the significance of Amanda’s imagined roles.
steps: List Amanda’s imagined characters (mermaid, orphan, Rapunzel, etc.)
Explain why she wants to be each character
Write 4–5 sentences connecting imagination to her longing for freedom
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will interpret figurative elements and psychological insight.

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification – Freedom vs Control
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the central theme of the poem.
steps: Highlight lines showing Amanda’s desire for freedom
Highlight lines showing parental control
Write a paragraph explaining how the poem balances these ideas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will identify theme and evaluate conflict between desire and restriction.

activities_projects.title: Advanced Character Sketch
activities_projects.objective: Develop a nuanced character sketch of Amanda.
steps: List words describing Amanda’s traits (e.g., imaginative, sensitive, rebellious)
Provide textual examples for each trait
Write a 6–7 sentence paragraph analyzing Amanda’s personality
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop analytical and interpretive skills for character study.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth, textual support, and clarity of description

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Expressive Reading
steps: Act out Amanda and the parent in pairs
Read lines with expression emphasizing emotions
Discuss how tone, pauses, and inflection affect meaning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will improve oral expression, interpretive reading, and empathy.
activities_projects.evaluation: Expression, participation, and understanding of character

activities_projects.title: Symbolism and Figurative Language
activities_projects.objective: Analyze imagery and figurative elements in Amanda’s daydreams.
steps: Identify figurative expressions in Amanda’s imagined scenarios
Explain what each image represents in terms of feelings or desires
Write a paragraph interpreting how imagination conveys deeper meaning
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recognize symbolism and figurative language in poetry.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Context Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Understand and use advanced words from the poem.
steps: Identify 6–7 new words from the poem (e.g., languid, sulking, contemptuous)
Write meanings and sentences for each
Explain how the word contributes to understanding Amanda or tone
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance vocabulary and apply words in context.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correctness of meaning, sentence formation, and relevance

activities_projects.title: Analytical Paragraph – Repetition
activities_projects.objective: Examine effect of repetition of Amanda’s name.
steps: Count occurrences of Amanda’s name
Explain why the poet repeats it and its impact on tone and focus
Write 4–5 sentences analysis
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will analyze stylistic techniques and interpret impact on reader.

activities_projects.title: Comparative Reflection Essay
activities_projects.objective: Relate Amanda’s experiences to personal life and other literature.
steps: Reflect on times when you felt restricted or daydreamed to escape
Compare with Amanda’s situation and describe feelings
Write a short essay connecting personal reflection to poem’s theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will develop reflective thinking and personal literary connections.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection, clarity, and relevance to text
